bids.reasoning: The opponent opened with their worst possible offer for me (all valueF = 0.20 utility on every issue), which is likely their ideal outcome - they want all valueF values. Their emotional message is cheap talk contradicting a very lopsided bid. Since we're at t=0.00, I should open with my ideal bid (all valueA) to maximize my utility and establish an anchor. There's no reason to concede at the very start.

It's very early in the negotiation (t=0.02). The opponent is repeatedly offering all valueF (worst for me, giving utility 0.2*0.4 + 0.2*0.3 + 0.2*0.2 + 0.2*0.1 = 0.20), and their emotional messages are cheap talk contradicted by their extreme bids. I should hold firm near my maximum utility. I'll maintain my top bid of all valueA, which gives me utility 1.0.

The opponent is making extremely low offers (all valueF, barely any movement), while using emotional appeals. Their actual bids give me very low utility (all valueF = 0.2 on each issue, weighted = 0.2 total). It's very early (t=0.04), so I should hold firm near my optimal position. I'll stay at my top utility bid since the opponent hasn't made meaningful concessions yet.

We're very early in the negotiation (t=0.06). The opponent is making extremely low offers (all valueF, slightly moving issueA to valueE), which gives me very low utility. Their emotional messages are cheap talk contradicted by their actual bids. I should stay firm near my optimal bid since there's plenty of time left. I'll maintain my top bid of all valueA across issues.

It's still very early (t=0.08). The opponent has been making extremely low offers (all valueF, with minor movement on one issue at a time). Their emotional messages don't match their bidding behavior - they're being very aggressive in trying to get the best values for themselves while appealing emotionally. I should stay firm near my top utility. I'll maintain my maximum bid for now, though I can make a very small concession on issueD (lowest weight) to show some willingness to move.

The opponent's offers have been consistently very low utility for me (all F values = 0.20 utility each, occasionally moving one issue to E or D). Their emotional messages are cheap talk contradicted by their stingy bidding behavior. At t=0.10, they offered issueA:valueD which is a slight improvement, but overall still very low utility for me. I'm still early in the negotiation, so I should maintain a strong position. I'll make a small concession from my maximum to signal willingness to negotiate while keeping utility high.

The opponent is making very small concessions - they've been mostly at valueF across all issues with tiny movements. Their emotional appeals are classic cheap talk that contradicts their lopsided bids (all valueF = minimum utility for me). It's still very early (t=0.12), so I should stay near my optimal bid. I'll hold firm on the most important issues (issueA, issueB) but make a small concession on lower-weight issues to signal willingness to negotiate.

The opponent is making very slow, small concessions - they're still offering mostly valueF/valueE across issues, which gives me very low utility. Their emotional messages are cheap talk; their actual bids are still heavily in their favor. At t=0.14, we're still early in the negotiation, so I should maintain a strong position. I'll make a small concession on issueD (my lowest weight issue) to signal willingness to negotiate, while staying firm on the higher-weight issues. I'll keep issueA, issueB at valueA (my top values) and make a slight move on issueC to valueB, and issueD to valueC to show some movement without giving away much utility.

The opponent is still offering very low utility to me (issueA: valueC, others: valueF gives me about 0.4*0.68 + 0.3*0.20 + 0.2*0.20 + 0.1*0.20 = 0.272+0.06+0.04+0.02 = 0.392). They're moving very slowly and their emotional appeals are cheap talk - their actual bids remain heavily skewed in their favor. At t=0.16, I should start making slight concessions but remain near my top-utility bids. I'll concede slightly on issueD (lowest weight) and issueC while holding firm on issueA and issueB.

The opponent has been making very slow concessions, mostly staying near valueF/valueE across all issues while I've been holding near my best offers. Their messages are emotional appeals but the actual bids remain very low utility for me. At t=0.18 we're still early, so I can afford to hold firm on issueA and issueB (my highest weights) while making small concessions on lower-weight issues to signal flexibility.
